parts.text:
**October 11th, 2031: The Party**

Okay, so I'm Dani Reyes. Junior at Whitmore, environmental science major. Kind of a mess right now, honestly. `This party is loud, Mel’s probably having a blast.` The request is clear: I'm going through something really difficult. I need to make the response narrative and from my perspective. No problem, I can do this. The details are important: October 11, 2031, Phi Delta Sigma party, and... well, it's not looking good. I have to make it feel real. Kayla's betrayal when I was fourteen still stings. I hate feeling like I don't have control. I can't let this happen again. I have to convey my thoughts as they happen.

I need to write this through a specific lens -- my messy life, the things I eat, my relationships. I need to get it right. I have to think about what I'm like when I'm under pressure. Dark wavy hair, biting the inside of my cheek when I think, that dad-inherited trait with my hands, hair tie on my wrist. I need to be careful, but I also make impulsive choices. `I hope I'm making the right choices tonight.` I eat oatmeal with peanut butter every morning. My roommate, Priya, who I love, is a social battery drain. Mel is my best friend. She's at OSU. I journal in my notes app. I probably overestimate my ability to read people, and maybe that's getting me into this situation. Okay, here we go.

<!-- Nervous, stomach churning -->

Upstairs. With him. `I shouldn't have come up here. Why did I come up here?` This isn't right. I don't want this. "No," I say. Firmly. `Please, listen.` He doesn't stop. `Dammit, dammit, dammit. He's not listening. Make him listen. This feels so familiar. I can’t let this happen.`

<!-- Panic rising, trying to stay calm but failing -->

He's not stopping. This isn't fair.

<!-- Feeling scared and angry and betrayed all at once-->

I hate this. I hate *him*. I need to get out of this.

<!-- Trying to focus. Trying to be strong. Trying to remember that I am not helpless. -->

I'm making a note of this later. Gotta remember.

<!-- Processing -->

**(Notes App - Later)**

*   It happened. Not the same, but it felt the same. No, *it* felt the same. I said no. He didn’t stop. I said it again. And again. I needed to get away. I did. It’s over now. But it isn't, is it? I feel sick. I am not okay. Remember: this wasn’t my fault.
